title: VP Harris pushes the envelope as Biden struggles with some Democrats
shortDescription: As left-leaning voters question Biden's age and leadership, Harris is taking on more heated topics, more often and more directly than Biden.

title: Critics slam Trump post with video online showing Biden hogtied
shortDescription: Trump sharing the clip underscores how nasty, personal the 2024 contest with Biden has become

title: Did this California legislative candidate storm the Capitol on Jan. 6?
shortDescription: Many candidates have run for office after having some role in the Capitol riot. Aguilar tells USA TODAY that she had 'no involvement.'

title: AZ GOP ballot initiative would scrap early voting, limit absentee ballots
shortDescription: The measure needs a vote of the full Senate to be sent to the November ballot, where voters would get the final say.

title: Trump returns to campaign trail as new poll shows Biden gaining
shortDescription: Trump has events scheduled in Green Bay, Wisc. and Grand Rapids, Mich. on April 2. He had been off the campaign trail for more than two weeks.

title: Doctored video shows Trump vowing to free Imran Khan | Fact check
shortDescription: The original clip from 2017 shows Trump talking to NBC News about FBI Director James Comey, not the former Pakistani prime minister.

title: Trump, others appeal decision to let Fani Willis stay on Georgia case
shortDescription: Trump and seven other defendants in the sweeping election fraud case say Willis and entire DA's office should be disqualified and the case thrown out

title: A look at when and how to vote in Alaska's upcoming elections
shortDescription: A look at when and how to vote in Alaska's upcoming elections

title: Gaza protests dim the glitz of Biden, Obama, Clinton gala in NYC
shortDescription: Gaza protesters inside and outside Biden's Radio City fundraiser served as a stark reminder of pressure Biden faces from the left and young voters.

title: Biden's student loan forgiveness, SAVE plan face further legal action
shortDescription: The Education Department last fall released its income-driven plan known as SAVE, which has canceled or reduced the balances of millions of borrowers.

title: Trump's New York fraud trial was civil, not criminal case | Fact check
shortDescription: The case is civil, not criminal, which means Trump was not facing charges. The post also oversimplifies the circumstances around the trial.

title: Bill for Malcolm X Day passes in Nebraska
shortDescription: The Nebraska Legislature passed a bill on Thursday that would honor the life and legacy of civil rights activist Malcolm X.

title: One year in a Moscow prison: Biden rallies for reporter Evan Gershkovich
shortDescription: President Joe Biden said he 'will continue to denounce and impose costs for Russia's appalling attempts to use Americans as bargaining chips.'

title: Gaza protesters disrupt Biden, Obama event
shortDescription: Pro-Palestinian protesters gathered outside President Biden's campaign event with former Presidents Obama and Clinton in New York.

title: Trump claims free speech protects him from prosecution | The Excerpt
shortDescription: Plus: the Army Corps of Engineers's view on how the Francis Scott Key bridge cleanup will happen. FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried sentenced to 25 years.

title: Biden says he will travel to Baltimore next week following bridge collapse
shortDescription: Latest on Baltimore bridge collapse: President Joe Biden on Friday said he will travel to Baltimore next week.

title: Obama and Clinton rip Trump, boost Biden at $26M NYC fundraiser
shortDescription: Barack Obama and Bill Clinton stumped for President Joe Biden in New York City Thursday night at the most lucrative fundraiser of the 2024 campaign.

title: Biden, Obama, Bill Clinton together at New York fundraiser
shortDescription: The fundraiser is expected to raise at least $10 million and draw at least 3,000 people. Guests who pay enough can have their portrait taken with the three presidents by world-renowned photographer Annie Leibovitz.

title: Judge rules Georgia Republican official illegally voted 9 times
shortDescription: Outspoken election denier Brian Pritchard insisted voter fraud cost former President Donald Trump the 2020 election. Now a judge has fined him $5,000.
